=== TECHNICAL SEO SETTINGS ===

Meta title: Custom Target Date Funds | PointOak Retirement Advisors

Meta description: Independent fiduciary evaluation and custom target date fund design for plan sponsors. 450+ data points reviewed per bidder. Schedule a consultation.

URL: https://www.pointoak.com/custom-target-date-funds/

Schema markup needed: Service, FAQPage, BreadcrumbList, HowTo

Custom Target Date Funds: Fiduciary Evaluation and Design

Most plans still run target date funds selected years ago without independent review. Target-date fund assets reached $4.0 trillion at year-end 2024, making the retirement asset allocation default a consequential decision. PointOak Retirement Advisors evaluates and designs custom target date funds for plan sponsors.

  • 450+ data points reviewed per plan bidder

  • Named 3(21) and 3(38) ERISA fiduciary

  • Fee-only since 2017, zero product commissions

Benefits of Custom Target Date Funds

Glide Path Aligned to Your Workforce

A packaged glide path assumes a generic investor profile. Custom target date fund design starts with your actual workforce: age distribution, salary bands, and retirement behavior. The resulting asset allocation is tailored to your plan's financial goals and risk tolerance.

Open-Architecture Manager Selection

Custom design gives your committee more control over manager selection across a wider variety of investments. DOL fiduciary guidance notes that non-proprietary options diversify portfolio exposure beyond a single provider. PointOak's investment analysis supports this open-architecture approach with flexibility to offer conservative and growth-oriented funds.

Fee Transparency Across Every Layer

Custom structures expose fees at every layer: wrapper, underlying fund, and overlay. Research in the Journal of Asset Management found that lower-expense-ratio funds outperformed same-vintage peers on average. Full fee visibility lets your committee adjust costs and use that insight to improve participant outcomes.

Reduced Single-Provider Concentration

Around 80% of total target-date assets sit with the five largest managers, per Morningstar. Most plans inherit that concentration by default. Custom target date solutions allow diversification across providers and reduce reliance on any single firm's proprietary lineup.

Participant Communication Matched to Plan Design

Off-the-shelf TDF marketing rarely explains the actual allocation strategy your participants hold. Customization extends to participant communications that describe the real glide path, not generic fund fact sheets. Accurate participant education reinforces confidence in the plan's default.

How Custom Target Date Funds Work

Custom target date fund design requires four committee-level decisions: glide path shape, asset class mix and manager selection, implementation vehicle (CIT vs. mutual fund), and rebalancing governance. PointOak's target date fund consulting structures each decision around the plan's invested allocations across multiple asset classes.

Vehicle choice carries material cost implications. Morningstar reported that collective investment trusts captured 52% of total target-date assets by mid-2024, a structural shift toward institutional pricing. For plans with sufficient scale, CITs reduce expense ratios on underlying holdings below what mutual fund share classes offer. This is a fiduciary consideration, not a detail to defer.

Schedule a consultation to evaluate whether a custom target date fund fits your plan.

Who Needs Custom Target Date Funds

Not every plan needs a custom target date fund. These signals suggest your plan is ready.

Your Current Default Has Never Been Benchmarked

If your plan's TDF was selected years ago without a documented comparison, the DOL's periodic review expectation may be unmet. Provider and fee benchmarking establishes the baseline.

Your Workforce Demographics Do Not Match Provider Assumptions

Wide age distribution, unusual salary bands, or high turnover create participant profiles that generic glide paths miss. A custom target date approach accounts for your plan's actual demographic experience rather than a provider's broad assumptions.

Your Plan Has Outgrown Its Off-the-Shelf Suite

Asset scale qualifies your plan for institutional vehicles and fee structures unavailable in packaged suites. Choosing to stay in a retail-priced lineup when CIT pricing is accessible costs participants directly over time.

Your Committee Wants Open-Architecture Manager Selection

Your committee seeks hands-on control over manager selection rather than accepting a single firm's lineup. This requires governance capacity for ongoing oversight and documented selection criteria.

A Recordkeeper Change Triggered a Default Review

A recordkeeper transition is a natural moment to evaluate whether the current default still fits. Treat the change as an opportunity to reassess your asset allocation strategy.

When Custom Might NOT Be the Right Fit

Smaller plans with limited committee bandwidth often benefit from a packaged suite's simplicity and lower governance burden. Custom adds value only when the plan's scale and complexity justify it.

Custom Target Date Funds vs. Packaged Target Date Funds

Custom target date solutions, packaged suites, and managed accounts each serve different plan profiles. The right choice depends on demographics, asset scale, and committee capacity.

Factor

Custom TDF

Packaged TDF

Glide path design

Configured to plan demographics

Standardized to broad market assumptions

Manager selection

Open-architecture, committee-selected

Provider's proprietary or curated lineup

Fee structure

Layered (wrapper + underlying); committee controls each

Bundled; simpler but less transparent

Governance burden

Higher; requires documented review cadence

Lower; largely provider-managed

Implementation vehicle

Can use CITs for institutional pricing

Typically mutual fund based

Sources: DOL, Morningstar, DCIIA

Factor

Custom TDF

Managed Account

Personalization level

Plan-level (workforce demographics)

Participant-level (individual data)

Default adoption

59% of participants in single TDF

7% of participants in managed account

Fee pressure

Institutional vehicle pricing available

Fees above 30 bps may require higher risk to match

Governance complexity

Committee oversees glide path and managers

Provider manages individual allocations

Communication clarity

Plan-wide messaging; consistent

Individualized; harder to communicate at scale

Sources: Vanguard's How America Saves 2025; industry consulting research

Custom fits plans with the assets, economic rationale, and committee bandwidth to justify ongoing oversight. Packaged suites deliver value through simplicity. An independent evaluation through fiduciary support helps your committee document its decision regardless of which structure fits.

What To Expect: Our Custom Target Date Fund Process

Step 1: Analyze Your Current Default

We review your existing TDF structure, fee layers, glide path, and participant demographics to establish a baseline.

Step 2: Evaluate Custom Fit

We assess whether your plan's demographics, asset scale, and committee capacity meet the threshold for custom implementation.

Step 3: Design and Recommend

You receive a documented recommendation with the rationale your fiduciary file requires.

Step 4: Implement and Monitor

We implement approved changes and continue as your point of contact through our retirement plan consulting services.

Why Choose PointOak for Custom Target Date Funds

According to PIMCO's 2025 DC Consulting Study, 80% of aggregators expect plan sponsors to increase blend target-date fund implementation. PointOak evaluates these shifts through independent research, not product inventory. Our clients receive insight on participant communications alongside the structural recommendation, ensuring your workforce understands how their invested assets are managed.

The concern that custom is vendor-driven is valid when the advisor earns commissions. As an independent, fee-only firm and named 3(21) and 3(38) ERISA fiduciary, PointOak treats no evaluation as a solicitation. We sell no proprietary funds and start every analysis with whether custom fits.

About PointOak Retirement Advisors

PointOak Retirement Advisors is an independent, fee-only wealth advisory firm founded in 2017 and headquartered in McLean, Virginia. SEC-registered investment advisor serving clients nationwide with over $1.08 billion in assets under management. Darsh Makim's practice dates to 2005, backed by 50+ years of collective team experience. Named 3(21) and 3(38) ERISA fiduciary. Schedule a complimentary consultation.

Frequently Asked Questions

What does custom target date fund implementation cost?

Cost depends on plan size, custom structure complexity, and the implementation vehicle selected.

Key factors include wrapper fees, underlying fund expenses, and the advisory fee for ongoing fiduciary oversight. DOL guidance directs fiduciaries to compare total costs and identify what services justify any fee differential. PointOak provides fee-layer analysis as part of every custom TDF evaluation, ensuring your committee has full visibility before committing money to a new structure.

How are custom target date funds different from managed accounts?

Custom target date funds adjust allocations at the plan level; managed accounts personalize at the participant level.

A custom TDF designs the glide path and selects managers for the entire workforce based on demographics. A managed account adjusts for each individual using personal data such as account balance and salary. PointOak evaluates both approaches to determine which default design serves your participants and their long-term financial life.

How often should a fiduciary committee review its custom target date fund?

Plan fiduciaries should review the custom TDF at least annually and document each review.

DOL guidance requires a documented periodic review process covering glide path performance, fee competitiveness, manager retention decisions, and participant outcome tracking. PointOak structures this review cadence as part of ongoing target date fund consulting engagements, so your committee has a clear schedule and fiduciary documentation for every cycle.

What fiduciary factors matter most when evaluating custom target date funds?

Glide path fit, total fee transparency, governance capacity, and manager selection discipline are the primary factors.

DOL fiduciary guidance for target-date funds names comparing and periodically reviewing TDF options as core fiduciary responsibilities. Committees should evaluate whether the glide path reflects actual participant demographics, whether fees are transparent at every layer, and whether governance resources support ongoing oversight. PointOak builds its evaluation around these aspects.

When should a plan sponsor consider a custom target date strategy?

Consider custom when your workforce demographics, plan scale, or existing lineup no longer fit a packaged suite.

Common signals include a wide age or salary spread across participants, institutional-scale assets that qualify for CIT pricing, or a committee seeking open-architecture manager selection. A recordkeeper transition is another trigger. PointOak's evaluation determines whether custom is appropriate before recommending any specific structure, so you get started with an honest assessment.

Does "custom" always mean lower fees?

Custom does not guarantee lower fees; some structures add cost layers that must be weighed against the benefits.

A custom target date fund may use lower-cost CITs or institutional share classes, but it can also introduce wrapper fees, manager overlay fees, or consulting costs absent in a packaged suite. The net cost impact depends on plan scale, vehicle, and architecture. Independent fee analysis is the only way to compare total value accurately.

When is a packaged target date fund the better choice?

A packaged suite often works better for smaller plans or committees without bandwidth for ongoing custom oversight.

Plans below a certain asset threshold may not access the institutional pricing that makes custom cost-effective. A committee without a regular meeting cadence may find the ongoing oversight difficult to sustain. Schedule a consultation with PointOak for a candid assessment of which structure serves your plan.

What role does the investment committee play in managing a custom target date fund?

The committee approves the glide path, selects managers, and documents each decision for the fiduciary file.

Custom target date funds add governance responsibilities beyond what a packaged suite requires. Your committee reviews manager performance, approves rebalancing changes, and maintains fiduciary documentation. PointOak structures this ongoing oversight to keep the fiduciary file current.

Contact PointOak at (703) 595-4444 or schedule a consultation to start your custom target date fund evaluation.
